matches.match_rationale: The core pain is not knowing which incentive types actually drive re-enrollment decisions. Propensity modeling can score each family's likelihood to re-enroll under different incentive conditions, identifying which discounts or offer types are predictive versus which just erode margin without moving behavior. For a multi-site operator, this directly replaces gut-feel incentive design with data-driven targeting.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A model trained on historical enrollment, incentive offer, and re-enrollment outcome data scores each family's re-enrollment probability with and without incentive, enabling the operator to selectively apply discounts only where they shift behavior — eliminating blanket offers that subsidize families who would have returned anyway.

matches.match_rationale: Discounts eroding margin without clear ROI is a direct pricing optimization problem — the operator is implicitly setting price floors through incentives without maximizing revenue. An AI pricing layer can optimize incentive depth and eligibility rules across sites to maximize re-enrollment rate per dollar of discount given, directly addressing the margin-erosion and inconsistency symptoms.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A yield management system that recommends site-specific incentive parameters (discount depth, eligibility cutoffs, early-bird windows) to maximize net tuition revenue across the portfolio, replacing ad-hoc or copy-paste offers with an optimized, centrally governed incentive structure.

matches.match_rationale: Families differ in their sensitivity to different incentive types (financial discount vs. sibling recognition vs. priority placement). Structured classification on family profile features — household size, tenure, payment history, prior engagement — can segment families into incentive-response archetypes, enabling targeted offer assignment rather than site-wide blanket policies.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A segmentation model that classifies enrolled families into incentive-response tiers at re-enrollment time, routing each family to the offer type most likely to convert them — financial discount, non-financial perk, or no incentive needed — reducing cost and inconsistency simultaneously.

matches.match_rationale: Families being unaware of available incentives is a communications failure. Personalized outreach generation can produce site-specific, family-tailored re-enrollment communications that surface the relevant incentive offer clearly and compellingly — replacing generic blast emails that families ignore with targeted messages tied to their specific situation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An LLM-powered communication layer that generates personalized re-enrollment letters and emails for each family, incorporating their applicable incentive offer, enrollment history, and campus-specific context — deployed consistently across all sites from a centrally governed template and offer logic.

matches.match_rationale: The operator has no data on which incentive types drive re-enrollment because they have never systematically tested them. Multi-armed bandit or Bayesian experiment design can structure ongoing incentive testing across sites to efficiently learn what works without requiring a formal RCT, turning the current chaos into an accelerating learning engine.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An adaptive experimentation platform that allocates different incentive variants across sites and cohorts, learns quickly which combinations drive re-enrollment at lowest cost, and converges toward the optimal offer mix — giving the operator an evidence base within one enrollment cycle.

matches.match_rationale: Inconsistent application of incentives across sites often stems from manual, decentralized processes where site admins apply offers ad hoc. A task automation agent can enforce offer eligibility rules, trigger the right incentive communications at the right time, and log every offer made — ensuring corporate incentive strategy is executed consistently without relying on individual site judgment.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An enrollment workflow agent that monitors re-enrollment status per family, applies configured eligibility logic to determine the correct offer, triggers personalized communications, and records outcomes — removing manual discretion from sites while maintaining central oversight of offer application.
